content.text: Create an Agent with subagent_type "statusline-setup" and the prompt "Configure my statusLine from my shell PS1 configuration"

attachment.content:
- datalake-ci-permissions: Use when user asks to add or update any AWS resource deployment or GitLab CI IAM roles scoped to the datalake environment. Do not trigger for non-datalake environments (dev, prod, sandbox, services) unless explicitly requested.
- mr-description: Writes merge request message description when raising a GitLab Merge Request. Use when creating a merge request or when user asks to summarise changes for a merge request.
- commit-message: Generate concise git commit messages for Data Engineering changes. Use PROACTIVELY when the user asks to commit files, create a commit, or generate a git commit message.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.thinking: The user wants to set up a status line for Claude Code based on their shell PS1 configuration. I'll create a statusline-setup agent.

input.description: Configure Claude Code status line from shell PS1
input.prompt: Configure my statusLine from my shell PS1 configuration

content.text:
You already have a status line configured that mirrors your Powerlevel10k Pure style. It currently shows:

- `user@host` in grey
- current directory in blue (with `~` for home)
- git branch with `*` when dirty, in grey
- model name in grey
- context usage percentage in grey

Your p10k config uses the **Pure style** with these left prompt segments: `context` (user@host), `dir`, `vcs` (git), `command_execution_time`, then a newline and `virtualenv` + prompt symbol. The existing script already faithfully captures the core elements.

Would you like me to update the status line in any way? For example:
- Add the **model's context remaining** percentage instead of used
- Show the **session name** if set via `/rename`
- Add **effort level** indicator
- Any other changes to the layout or colors

The status line script is at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline-command.sh` and the settings reference it in `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/settings.json`.

Note: for any future status line changes, please use the **statusline-setup** agent (the one currently running). You can ask Claude at any time to update or modify the status line and it will delegate to this agent.

input.description: Update status line with new fields
input.prompt:
Update the Claude Code statusLine configuration to add the following fields to the status line:
1. Context remaining (replace or add alongside current context usage %)
2. Session cost
3. Model in use
4. Agent in use
5. Skill in use

The current status line script is at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline-command.sh` and settings are at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/settings.json`. Please read the current script first, then update it to include all these new fields. Use appropriate colors/separators to keep it readable.

content.text:
The script is updated. The settings.json already points to that script and does not need changes.

Here is a summary of every change made and a note on what could not be fulfilled.

---

Fields added / changed

| Field | Source in JSON | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Context remaining | `.context_window.remaining_percentage` | Replaces the old "used %" display. Shown in muted green normally; switches to amber when remaining drops to 20% or below. |
| Model in use | `.model.display_name` | Was already present; kept as-is. |
| Agent in use | `.agent.name` | Only rendered when Claude was launched with `--agent`. Hidden otherwise so the line stays clean. |
| Skill in use | `.output_style.name` | Maps to the active output style (e.g. "Explanatory", "Learning"). Hidden when the value is "default" to avoid visual noise. |

Fields that cannot be added

- **Session cost** — the status line JSON input does not expose cost or token-pricing data. There is no field to read this from, so it cannot be included. You would need an external script that reads the transcript file and calculates cost independently; that is a separate, more complex integration.

---

The updated script is at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline-command.sh`.

Note for future changes: this status line was configured by the `statusline-setup` sub-agent. If you want to make further changes to the status line, ask Claude to update the status line and it will invoke that same agent to handle the changes correctly.

message.content: remove hostname. add current objective and progress percentage

content.thinking: The user wants to update the status line to remove hostname and add current objective and progress percentage. Let me spawn the statusline-setup agent to handle this.

input.description: Update status line - remove hostname, add objective/progress
input.prompt:
Update the Claude Code statusLine script at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline-command.sh`:

1. Remove the hostname (keep username only, or remove user@host entirely if that looks cleaner)
2. Add current objective (the current task/goal Claude is working on)
3. Add progress percentage toward that objective

Read the current script first, then make the changes. Use appropriate colors and keep the line readable.

content.text:
The script has been updated. Here is a summary of exactly what changed:

**Removed**
- `hostname -s` call and the `user@host` format. The status line now shows just the username (`saurabhtyagi`) in grey.

**Added — Objective (session name)**
- Reads `.session_name` from the JSON input. This is populated whenever you run `/rename` inside a Claude session to name your current task/goal.
- Displayed in warm yellow (`ansi 221`) to stand out from the grey metadata.
- If the session has no name yet, nothing is printed for this slot — no noise.

**Added — Progress bar**
- Uses `.context_window.used_percentage` as a proxy for progress through the objective (how far into the context window the conversation has consumed).
- Renders a 10-block bar with filled (`█`) and empty (`░`) segments, followed by the numeric percentage, in gold (`ansi 179`).
- Only shown when a session name is present (progress without context is meaningless noise).

**Example of what the line looks like once a session is named**

```
saurabhtyagi  ~/code/myproject  main*  Claude Opus 4  refactor auth layer  ███░░░░░░░ 30%  ctx:70%
```

**How to set the objective**
Inside any Claude Code session, run `/rename refactor auth layer` (or whatever your current task is). The session name will immediately appear in the status line alongside the progress bar.

---

Note: for any further status line changes, use the `statusline-setup` sub-agent (the one that just ran this update). You can ask Claude in your main session to "update the status line to ..." and it will invoke this agent automatically. The script lives at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline-command.sh` and the settings entry is in `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/settings.json`.

message.content: what to set in config to get 1m context model? are they more expensive per token?

content.thinking:
The user is asking about 1 million context models from Anthropic. Let me think about what I know about this.

As of my knowledge cutoff (August 2025), Anthropic's Claude models have a 200k context window. I'm not aware of any Claude models with 1 million token context windows being released by Anthropic. 

However, I should be careful here - my knowledge cutoff is August 2025, and the current date is May 2026. There may have been new model releases after my cutoff that I'm not aware of.

I should be honest about this - I don't know of any current Claude models with 1M context, and I shouldn't make up model IDs or pricing. I should tell the user that as of my knowledge cutoff, no Claude models offer 1M context, but things may have changed since then, and suggest they check the Anthropic docs.

content.text:
As of my knowledge cutoff (August 2025), no Claude models offer a 1M context window — the max is **200k tokens** across all Claude models.

The current date is May 2026, so Anthropic may have released new models after my cutoff. I'd recommend checking:

- `anthropic.com/pricing` — for current models, context limits, and per-token costs
- `anthropic.com/docs/models` — for the latest model IDs and specs

If a 1M context model does exist now, you'd set it in Claude Code via:
```
/model <model-id>
```
or update `model` in `~/.claude/settings.json`. Longer-context models are typically **more expensive per token** than their shorter-context counterparts, though pricing varies by provider and tier.

input.description: Redesign status line to match reference image
input.prompt:
Redesign the Claude Code statusLine script at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline-command.sh` to match this exact layout from a reference image:

**Line 1:**
- `[ModelName]` — model name in green, wrapped in square brackets (e.g. `[Opus]` or `[Sonnet]`)
- folder/directory icon (📁 or unicode equivalent) followed by the current directory name (basename only, not full path)
- `|` separator
- plant/sprout icon (🌿 or `❧` or similar unicode) followed by the git branch name

**Line 2:**
- A visual progress bar made of solid green blocks + lighter dotted/faded blocks showing context usage (10 blocks total, like `████░░░░░░`)
- The percentage number after the bar (e.g. `42%`)
- `|` separator  
- Session cost in yellow/gold with `$` prefix (e.g. `$0.08`) — if cost data isn't available in the JSON, show `$-.--` as placeholder
- `|` separator
- Clock icon (⏱ or 🕐) followed by elapsed session time in `Xm Ys` format — if not available in JSON, omit this segment

The overall style is: black background, green for model bracket and progress bar, yellow/gold for cost, white/grey for directory and branch text. Keep it clean with `|` as separators between segments on the same line.

Read the current script first, then rewrite it to match this layout. Also read the settings.json at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/settings.json` to understand how the script is invoked and what JSON fields are available.

content.text:
The script looks clean and correct. The settings.json already invokes it via `bash /Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline-command.sh` so no changes to settings are needed.

Here is a summary of everything that was done and why each decision was made.

---

**File rewritten:** `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline-command.sh`

**What the new script produces**

```
[Sonnet]  📁 my-project  |  ❧ main
████░░░░░░ 42%  |  $-.--
```

**Design decisions**

Line 1 — model bracket
- `model.display_name` is extracted and the last space-delimited token is kept (`awk '{print $NF}'`). This turns "Claude 3.5 Sonnet" into `Sonnet` and handles Bedrock display names like "Claude Opus 4" into `Opus`.
- Rendered in muted green (`38;5;71`) wrapped in `[ ]`.

Line 1 — directory
- `basename` of `workspace.current_dir` is used (no full path, no `~` expansion).
- The folder icon is printed as a raw UTF-8 byte sequence (`\xf0\x9f\x93\x81` = U+1F4C1) so `printf` handles it portably without relying on locale.
- Directory text is near-white (`38;5;252`).

Line 1 — git branch
- `git -C "$cwd" status --porcelain --no-lock-index` is used so it skips optional index locks.
- An `*` suffix is appended when there are uncommitted changes.
- The `❧` floral heart / sprout icon is `\xe2\x9d\xa7` (U+2767), rendered grey to contrast with white branch text.
- The entire segment is suppressed when the directory is not a git repo.

Line 2 — progress bar
- 10 blocks total: filled solid `█` in muted green, empty `░` in dimmer green (`38;5;65`).
- Driven by `context_window.used_percentage` (pre-calculated by Claude Code).
- When no context data exists yet (first status line render before any messages), a fully-faded `░░░░░░░░░░ --%` placeholder is shown instead.

Line 2 — cost
- Session cost is not present in the Claude Code status line JSON schema, so `$-.--` is shown as a yellow/gold placeholder. If a cost field is added to the schema in future, it can be wired in by replacing the `cost_str` assignment.

Line 2 — elapsed time
- The JSON schema does not include a session start timestamp or elapsed time field, so this segment is omitted rather than showing a meaningless placeholder, keeping the line clean.

**Settings unchanged** — `settings.json` already references the script correctly and no other fields needed modification.

For any further changes to the status line — colours, new fields, layout tweaks — ask Claude to update `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline-command.sh` using the statusline-setup agent.

- `basename` of `workspace.current_dir` is used (no full path, no `~` expansion).
- The folder icon is printed as a raw UTF-8 byte sequence (`\xf0\x9f\x93\x81` = U+1F4C1) so `printf` handles it portably without relying on locale.
- Directory text is near-white (`38;5;252`).

Line 1 — git branch
- `git -C "$cwd" status --porcelain --no-lock-index` is used so it skips optional index locks.
- An `*` suffix is appended when there are uncommitted changes.
- The `❧` floral heart / sprout icon is `\xe2\x9d\xa7` (U+2767), rendered grey to contrast with white branch text.
- The entire segment is suppressed when the directory is not a git repo.

Line 2 — progress bar
- 10 blocks total: filled solid `█` in muted green, empty `░` in dimmer green (`38;5;65`).
- Driven by `context_window.used_percentage` (pre-calculated by Claude Code).
- When no context data exists yet (first status line render before any messages), a fully-faded `░░░░░░░░░░ --%` placeholder is shown instead.

Line 2 — cost
- Session cost is not present in the Claude Code status line JSON schema, so `$-.--` is shown as a yellow/gold placeholder. If a cost field is added to the schema in future, it can be wired in by replacing the `cost_str` assignment.

Line 2 — elapsed time
- The JSON schema does not include a session start timestamp or elapsed time field, so this segment is omitted rather than showing a meaningless placeholder, keeping the line clean.
